message:
[medium] clickup: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

validate_base_url_for_auth pinned the host to api.clickup.com in
direct-token mode and accepted any https URL in credential_id mode, but
returned parsed.to_string().trim_end_matches('/') — query, fragment,
and userinfo components survived into ClickUpClient which concatenates
via format!("{}{path}", self.base_url) in every request method
(client.rs:177/188/200). A configure payload like

    {"api_token": "pk_x", "base_url": "https://api.clickup.com/api/v2?leak=x"}

would pass validation and leak `?leak=x` on every ClickUp API request
with the endpoint path parsed as part of the query. Userinfo
(`https://attacker:pw@api.clickup.com/...`) would bake into every
request URL and silently override the Authorization header.

- validate_base_url_for_auth now rejects userinfo (username or password)
  and query/fragment components before the host-allowlist / scheme
  checks, in both ApiToken and CredentialId modes.
- Six new unit tests: accept clean api.clickup.com (happy path), Token
  reject (query / fragment / userinfo), CredentialId reject (query),
  and a substring-smuggle regression verifying
  `https://evil.com/api.clickup.com/...` is rejected because the
  parsed host is evil.com.

Verification: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo check -p
fcp-clickup --tests is blocked by an upstream asupersync main
regression at src/runtime/waker.rs:128 (duplicate `use std::task::Wake;`
E0252, already imported at line 13). That crate is outside this pane's
domain — same blocker pattern noted in 6bcbdae8 / airtable. ClickUp
diff reviewed manually: additions are std url::Url accessor calls
returning standard shapes with error shapes identical to the
surrounding validator branches. Variants `ClickUpAuth::ApiToken` and
`ClickUpAuth::CredentialId` verified at connector.rs:54-55.
Structurally identical to the hubspot patch committed in b69cfcf5
this session, which compiled green and passed 213/213 lib tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
